title: Checkout totals off for discounted orders after overnight deploy
description:
## Summary

Support flagged this first thing this morning after a deploy went out around 2:47 AM. We are seeing bad totals in the orders API, but only on carts with discounts applied.

### What people are seeing

- Discounted orders are coming out a little high
- Some percentage discounts are off by a cent
- Orders without a promo code still look normal

### Example from support

- 2x Widget at $25.00 = $50.00 subtotal
- Promo code: SAVE10
- Tax: 8%
- Expected total: $48.60
- Actual total: $49.14

We have a couple of similar reports where percentage discounts on values like $49.99 round down to $4.99 instead of $5.00.

### Notes so far

- Discount config looks fine
- Tax config looks fine
- This seems to have started with last night's deploy
- The pricing flow changed recently as part of the regional pricing work in GitHub issue #12
- GitHub issues #15 and #18 have notes from earlier debugging

### Impact

Customers are being overcharged, support is issuing refunds manually, and we should assume this is customer-visible until proven otherwise.

### Done when

- Discounted totals line up with the expected math
- Percentage and flat discounts both behave correctly
- Root cause is fixed and a PR is up
